title: Bulbophyllum citricolor
taxonomy: Bulbophyllum citricolor is a species of orchid. It belongs to the family Orchidaceae. The genus Bulbophyllum is a large and diverse group of orchids, comprising over 2,000 species. Bulbophyllum citricolor is classified within the subtribe Malaxideinae, which is a group of terrestrial orchids. Within the subtribe, Bulbophyllum citricolor is further classified into the section Bulbophyllum sect. Bulbophyllum.

intro: Bulbophyllum citricolor, also known as the citrus orchid, is a species of orchid native to the tropical regions of Madagascar. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, making it a promising natural remedy for various ailments. The bioactive constituents of Bulbophyllum citricolor include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
